markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Assemble snippets with source paths, spans, hashes, extraction receipts, and unsupported-claim markers. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: EvidenceSnippetAssembler leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EvidenceSnippetAssembler is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified dd02de8d; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: A span with a deleted source was still marked supported=true
evidence.fix: Snippet marks supported=false with explicit reason when the source row is missing; noted the FK law (spans CASCADE on source delete) already makes the state unrepresentable - defensive closure

findings.title: a span with a deleted source is still marked supported
findings.evidence: assemble_span_snippet (snippet.rs:77-95): when the source row is gone, source_path=None but supported=true. supported reflects span existence only, not source availability.
findings.fix: Optionally flag source-missing as a soft unsupported/degraded marker.

adversarial_review_v2.note: Carries span range + content_sha256 + extraction receipt + source path; explicit supported=false + reason for missing span / claim-with-no-spans (anti-hallucination honored); real-span citation proven (pipeline_tests:216).

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-135 requires an EvidenceSnippetAssembler that emits snippets carrying source path, span range, content hash, extraction-receipt EventLedger id, and explicit unsupported-claim markers, backed by real PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The committed code (snippet.rs) implements exactly this, is wired into the live retrieval executor, reads through the committed KnowledgeStore against real PG, and is proven by real-PG integration tests for supported, source-missing, and ghost/unsupported paths. It aligns with current Master Spec v02.194 section 2.3.13.11.
findings: snippet.rs:56-106 assemble_span_snippet loads the span and its source through the real PostgresDatabase (KnowledgeStore), producing EvidenceSnippet with source_path, range_start/end, line_start/end, content_sha256, extraction_receipt_event_id, and excerpt — every field the MT scope names.

snippet.rs:60-75 a missing span yields supported=false with reason 'span not found in index' rather than an error; snippet.rs:82-90 a span whose source row is gone yields supported=false with reason 'the span's source no longer exists' (the adversarial-v2 LOW closure cited in lifecycle.evidence, commit a46b9e6b). Defensive belt under the FK CASCADE law.

snippet.rs:112-158 assemble_claim_snippets: a claim with no backing evidence spans returns a single supported=false unsupported-claim marker rather than dropping it — directly satisfies the spec anti-hallucination requirement (Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md:2701,2704: KnowledgeSpan is the minimum citeable evidence unit and claims MUST carry evidence spans).
snippet.rs:163-179 citation() emits stable 'path:lines@sha8' for supported and '...@UNSUPPORTED' for unsupported — the visible-citation contract.
executor.rs:243-245 wires assemble_span_snippet into the live retrieval executor path (not a dead helper), so the assembler runs in the real bundle-compilation pipeline.

Real-PG proof, not mock-only: knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:368-377 (supported span citation incl. content hash + path), :1212-1215 (ghost span KSP-0000... asserts !supported), knowledge_retrieval_replay_tests.rs:69-73 (supported + unsupported snippet replay). All run against fx.pg.db from MemoryFixture::setup() which uses knowledge_pg() — fixtures header states 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks.' Gated by skip_if_no_pg! so they exercise real managed PG when available.
sub_rule_1: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/placeholder/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' anywhere in snippet.rs or the executor wiring reachable from the required behavior.
sub_rule_3 NA: lifecycle.claimed_by and completed_by are both null; no implementer self-certification of the COMPLETED transition.

The in-module #[cfg(test)] tests in snippet.rs:182-224 only test the citation() formatter with hand-built structs (not PG) — but that does not weaken the verdict because the real PG-backed proof lives in the integration tests above; the unit tests are a thin formatting supplement, not the authority proof.
Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet cites v02.192 anchors; resolved against current v02.194 where the same section content exists — not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show dd02de8d (feat MT-135 snippet.rs) and a46b9e6b (LOW sweep) in wtc-kernel-009

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
